prompt_text: Retool vs Budibase pricing and features
response:
**Budibase** is generally the cheaper, more open option, especially if you want **self-hosting** or an **open-source** platform, while **Retool** is usually the stronger choice for teams that want a more mature developer-focused tool with a larger component and integration ecosystem.[1][2][4]  

| Category | **Budibase** | **Retool** |
|---|---|---|
| **Pricing model** | Free self-hosted; paid cloud/enterprise options start around **$5/user/month** or **$19/month starter** depending on plan/source[1][5] | Free tier; paid plans start around **$10/user/month** or, in some comparisons, **$299/month** for higher tiers[1][3][5] |
| **Open source / self-hosting** | **Open source** and supports **self-hosting**[1][2] | **Proprietary**; cloud and on-prem/self-hosted options are available[2] |
| **Primary audience** | More accessible for **non-developers** and mixed teams[1] | Built for **developers** and technical teams[1][2] |
| **Ease of use** | Simpler visual builder; more approachable for forms and CRUD apps[1][4] | More powerful but typically requires more SQL/API/JavaScript knowledge[1][2] |
| **Core strengths** | Open-source, built-in database, cost-effectiveness, self-host control[1][2] | Large component library, strong API/database integrations, mature internal-tool workflow[1][2] |
| **Typical best fit** | Internal tools, forms, CRUD workflows, budget-conscious teams[1][4] | Complex internal tools for technical teams needing speed and flexibility[1][4] |

- **Choose Budibase** if your priority is lower cost, open-source control, or self-hosting.[1][2]
- **Choose Retool** if your priority is developer productivity, richer integrations, and a more established internal-tools platform.[1][2][4]

There is some **pricing inconsistency across comparison sources**, so the safest interpretation is that **Budibase’s entry cost is lower overall**, while **Retool’s paid plans can become more expensive as usage scales**.[1][3][5]
